raw_consumer_statement:
On XX/XX/year>, a charge of {$660.00} from XXXX XXXX a Car ( XXXX, XXXX ) was posted to my Citibank credit card for alleged damage to a rental vehicle. 
The merchant claimed damage to the drivers side rear light. This damage was pre-existing and present before the rental began. I recorded video at vehicle pickup clearly showing the same damage prior to driving the car. 
Between XX/XX/XXXX and XX/XX/year>, I contacted the merchant multiple times by email, provided the video evidence, and requested removal of the charge. The merchant did not respond within their stated XXXX hour timeframe and did not provide any documentation showing the damage occurred during my rental. 
I opened a dispute with Citibank and submitted video evidence, email correspondence showing lack of merchant response, the rental contract, and a written explanation. Citibank issued a conditional credit during the investigation. 
On XX/XX/XXXX, Citibank denied the dispute and reversed the conditional credit, stating that the documentation did not support the claim. Citibank did not explain why the video evidence was insufficient, did not address the pre-existing damage, and evaluated the dispute under an incorrect category ( services not as expected ) rather than as an invalid or unauthorized damage charge. 
I am requesting a supervisory review and proper reconsideration of this dispute based on the evidence submitted and the merchants failure to substantiate the charge.
